t_en: Ah...

t_ch: 啊啊啊……
t_en: Ah...

t_ch: <size=36>啊！这脸庞！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Ah! The face!</size>

t_ch: <size=36>啊！这鼻子！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Ah! The nose!</size>

t_ch: <size=36>啊！这眼睛！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Ah! The eyes!</size>

t_ch: 希露瓦大人真的…太完美了！简直可以与大守护者相媲美……
t_en: Lady Serval truly is... just too perfect! She's as beautiful as the Supreme Guardian...

t_ch: 我只得庆幸此刻还活着！只因我能有幸看到如此完美的事物！
t_en: I'm so lucky to be alive now! I've had the opportunity to see something so perfect!

t_ch: 救命！我感觉…希露瓦大人已经令我心律不齐了…我要把希露瓦大人的玉照挂在我的床头…这样我就能天天看着我的希露瓦大人了！
t_en: Save me! I think... Lady Serval has caused my heart to skip several beats... I want to hang Lady Serval's picture on my headboard... That way I can see her every day!

t_ch: 有缘再见了，我的同好！我要回去静养了…愿我们下次相遇时……
t_en: Until we meet again, my fellow fan! I need to go and recover... I hope that the next time we meet...

t_ch: <size=36>我们都能拥有希露瓦大人新的玉照！</size>
t_en: <size=36>We all can have a new picture of Lady Serval!</size>

t_ch: 我还没有。
t_en: Not yet.

t_ch: 那么…就拜托你了！
t_en: So... I'll leave that to your capable hands!

t_ch: 啊…她真美丽……
t_en: Ah... She is truly beautiful...

t_ch: 我的希露瓦大人…！
t_en: My Lady Serval...!

t_ch: 胜券在握…
t_en: Victory is at hand!

t_ch: 多么完美的棋局，专业的棋手敢于下判断…你在干什么喀！？
t_en: What a perfect game. The professional player levels their judgement with courage and... what are you doing!?

t_ch: 红色方明明大好局势啊…我解说不下去了喀！
t_en: The red side clearly has a major advantage... I can't go on with my commentary!

t_ch: 这条路真是漫长啊喀。我已经连续爬了不知多久，却还是没到终点喀。
t_en: This pathway really does go on and on. I've been climbing goodness knows how long, but still haven't hit the end yet.

t_ch: …传奇的旅途注定艰辛，看来我还得继续努力喀。
t_en: ...Legendary voyages are destined to be difficult. Looks like I'll have to keep working hard at it.

t_ch: 年轻的战士啊，为何你要选择回头喀？难道说，你还有所留恋嘛……
t_en: Young warrior there: Why do you choose to turn back, crrk? Could it be you're still reluctant to leave...

t_ch: 出征没有回头路，正如纷争一旦挑起就没法轻易平息。这是我们的命运喀……
t_en: Once you go to war, there's no turning back, just as there is no way to calm things down easily once a dispute arises. And so, this is our fate...

t_ch: 喀喀——要说设计建筑，最重要就三点：公平、公平，还是公平。
t_en: Crrk crrk — When it comes to building design, there are three things to consider: Fairness, fairness, and fairness.

t_ch: 就像天秤的两端，倘若楼梯另一侧有宝箱，那这一侧也必须有东西才行…比如一只虫！
t_en: Just like the two ends of a scale. Suppose there is a treasure on one side of the stairs then so, too, must there be something on this side... like a bug!

t_ch: 早就听说持矛君王是个坏家伙，今天我倒要跟它斗上一斗，看看谁才是最猛的喀。
t_en: I heard the Spearlord is one bad egg. I want to fight against them — today — to see who's the baddest of the bad.

t_ch: 可我都在这等了这么久，它却还是迟迟没有出现。难道被我的威名吓住了喀？
t_en: But I've been waiting here all this time, and they still haven't shown up. Could they be cowering in awe at my reputation?

t_ch: 喀…那是持矛君王派来的侍者吗？好可怕喀……
t_en: Crrk... Is that who the Spearlord sent over? Super scary...

t_ch: 既然我拥有翅膀，那就理应向天空发起挑战。我要把苍穹之眼摘下来喀！
t_en: Seeing as I have wings, I should be issuing a challenge to the sky: I vow to pluck out the eye of the heavens above!

t_ch: 是时候迈出虫生的第一步了…怎么回事，我张不开翅膀喀！
t_en: It's time to take the first step in my bug life... What's going on — I can't open up my wings!

t_ch: 喀喀——人无法两次踏入相同的河流，也无法仅凭火光观测洞穴外的世界……
t_en: Crrk crrk — One can never set foot in the same river twice. Nor can one ever observe the world beyond the cave by torchlight alone...

t_ch: …若要抵达真实的对岸，必须先找到通往真实的路才行喀。
t_en: ...If you wish to arrive at the other side of truth, you must first find the pathway leading towards the truth.

t_ch: 唉，我和其他虫都聊不来喀。只有你，我的朋友，愿意倾听我的诉说……
t_en: *sigh* I can't really talk to other bugs. Only you, my friend, are willing to hear me out...

t_ch: 谁说你是失败品的喀！如果你是失败品，那只能和失败品为伍的我又算什么呢喀？
t_en: Who said you're a failure! If you're a failure, then what the heck does that make me, who can only be friends with failures?

t_ch: 看见那火了吗？它大概多久燃起一次？
t_en: See that fire over there? How often does it light up?

t_ch: 两秒？
t_en: Every 2 seconds?

t_ch: 四秒？
t_en: Every 4 seconds?

t_ch: 八秒？
t_en: Every 8 seconds?

t_ch: 我不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 感谢你如此认真地回答。答案是——我也不知道，因为我根本没有能记数的手指头喀！
t_en: Thank you for your earnest guess. The correct answer is: I don't know either, because I don't have any fingers to count on!

t_ch: 喀喀——你知道么，建筑的设计会影响访客的心态。而面前这道沟壑影响了我的心态……
t_en: Crrk crrk — Did you know that a building's design can affect the mood of its visitors? And this gorge before me is impacting my mood...

t_ch: 究竟是谁设计出来的？到底要怎么过去啊喀！
t_en: Who designed this? How do we get through!

t_ch: 喀喀——吾王剑锋所指，就是吾等心之所向。如此霸气的君王，还有什么能抵挡得了？
t_en: Crrk crrk — Wheresoever my master's sword points, that is where my heart rests. What can stop such a mighty king?

t_ch: …不对，我要效忠的主人似乎并不用剑？有些记不清了喀……
t_en: ...No, no, I don't think the master I serve wields a sword? I can't quite remember...

t_ch: 喀！据说雕像所刻的这种怪物曾经真实存在，但我从来没见到过活着的…难道是都被战士们清缴了喀？
t_en: Crrk! Legend has it that the mythical beast carved by that statue actually existed once, but I've never seen a live one before... I guess warriors must've wiped them all out?

t_ch: 多亏了各位战士，我们才能生活在一个更加安全的世界喀。
t_en: Praise be to those warriors, who've provided a safer world for us to live in.

t_ch: 喀喀——这就是虫的一生，一遍遍重复那些徒劳的事，一次次把石头推上陡坡……
t_en: Crrk crrk — That's the life of a bug: Repeating fruitless labors over and over again, rolling that stone up the hill over and over again...

t_ch: 没有人可以将我从这份职责中解放…但如果你能帮我把石头推回去的话就再好不过了喀。
t_en: There's nobody who can free me from this duty... But it sure would be great if you could help me push the stone back.

t_ch: 喀喀——桥梁对城邦来说必不可少，正如再伟大的君王也会需要逃生密道。倘若我们彼此隔绝，「死亡」的脚步也会接踵而至。
t_en: Crrk crrk — Bridges are vital to a city. Just like how even the greatest ruler still needs a secret escape route, if we become isolated from each other, then the steps of "Death" shall swiftly follow.

t_ch: 这便是「雅努斯秘径」的伟大之处。那些分散的终将连结为一，无论自愿与否……
t_en: That's the beauty of the "Janus's Hidden Passage." The separated will all eventually be connected into one, whether they like it or not...

t_ch: 完美的角度！精确的设计！我已经测量过许多遍了，这次一定能成喀！
t_en: The perfect angle! Exquisite design! I've measured it a dozen times over, and this time is gonna be the one!

t_ch: 现在只差一个推动巨石的触发装置了，艺术就是碾压喀！
t_en: Now all I need is a trigger to push the boulder. My art would really be "crushing it"!

t_ch: 哦哦哦，动起来了，动起来了喀！
t_en: Whoa, it's moving! It's really moving!

t_ch: 完美的角度！精确的设计！艺术就是碾压喀！
t_en: The perfect angle! Exquisite design! Crushing is an art form!

t_ch: 听好了喀，这是一段被掩盖起来的故事，只有最智慧的人才能知晓答案——「浪漫」与「理性」两位泰坦是何关系？
t_en: Listen up, and I'll tell you a story about a cover-up that only the wisest people know about: What is the relationship between the Titans of Romance and Reason?

t_ch: 造物与创造者。
t_en: Creation and creator.

t_ch: 学生与老师。
t_en: Student and teacher.

t_ch: 友人兼恋人。
t_en: Friends and lovers.

t_ch: 以上都是。
t_en: All of the above.

t_ch: 回答正确！你发现了卵石下的青苔——不要把它和别人分享。
t_en: Correct! You discovered the hidden beauty here — now don't share it with anyone else.

t_ch: 噗啊——总算能喘口气了喀。为了跟那些石雕像比拼耐力，我已经保持不动好久了喀……
t_en: *puff* Finally, a chance to catch my breath. I've been standing dead still for ages in this competition of endurance against those other statues...

t_ch: 没想到你一进入这房间，它们就都动了起来…赢得还真是轻松呢喀。
t_en: I didn't know they'd all start moving as soon as you walked into the room... What an easy win.

t_ch: 冠军也有我的三分之一。
t_en: Sounds like one-third of that win belongs to me.

t_ch: 喀！这么说倒也不算错喀……
t_en: Crrk! When you put it like that, you're not exactly wrong...

t_ch: 可你根本不会动啊。
t_en: But you can't move at all.

t_ch: 喀喀！怎么能这么说！在你看不到的地方，我们动得可欢了！
t_en: Crrk crrk — How could you say such a thing! We're jiggling around happily when you aren't looking!

t_ch: 总之，多亏了你，这场比赛是我的胜利喀！
t_en: Anyway, I won this contest all thanks to you!

t_ch: 喀喀，听我指挥——加入智慧、勇武、迅捷和刚毅……
t_en: Crrk crrk — Listen carefully: Add intellect, fortitude, speed, and resolve...

t_ch: 加入刚毅，再加入刚毅！继续加，不要停喀！
t_en: Add more resolve, and add more again! Keep adding that, don't stop!

t_ch: 喀喀——看到这些堆叠的石料了吗？它们都是重要的物资，必须好好清点喀。
t_en: Crrk crrk — See that stack of rocks over there? They're all really important materials and need to be carefully counted.

t_ch: 等战争结束后，大家就能用它们盖更多更大的屋子，到时候再也不会有人挨冻了喀。
t_en: Once the war is over, everyone can build bigger houses out of them, and nobody will ever go cold again.

t_ch: 我、我看见了一幅可怕的景象：黑暗吞噬了太阳，辉煌的宫殿变成了废墟，一切都在不可避免地滑入深渊……
t_en: I... I have seen a terrifying image: Darkness engulfing the sun. A glorious palace reduced to ruins. Everything sliding inexorably towards this void...

t_ch: 那是怎样孤独的感觉啊！简直就像古人第一次仰望夜空，却发现所有的星星都已经熄灭……
t_en: What a lonely sensation! It's like the ancients gazing up to the stars for the first time only to find them all extinguished...

t_ch: 阳光依旧明媚…一定是梦喀。
t_en: But the sun still beams down brightly... It must have all been a dream.

t_ch: 根本没有火！
t_en: There's no fire at all!

t_ch: 什么？居然真的没有了，这可是大凶之兆啊！怎么会这样喀……
t_en: What? It's really gone? This is an omen of great woe! How could this have happened...

t_ch: 怎么还没到头…
t_en: Why is there no end in sight...

t_ch: 不要回头…
t_en: Don't turn back...

t_ch: 平等之光在闪耀！
t_en: The light of equality shines!

t_ch: 这就是它的大本营？
t_en: This is their base camp?

t_ch: 我会飞！
t_en: I can fly!

t_ch: 并非此刻
t_en: Not this moment

t_ch: 并非真实
t_en: Not reality

t_ch: 其他虫都不和我玩…
t_en: The other nymphs don't want to play with me...

t_ch: 火焰是最棒的…
t_en: Fire is the best...

t_ch: 我桥呢？
t_en: Where's my bridge?

t_ch: 征服世界！
t_en: World conquest, here I come!

t_ch: 这世上曾经有牛吗…
t_en: Were there cows in this world...

t_ch: 虫生是无尽的苦旅…
t_en: The life of a nymph is endless toiling...

t_ch: 必须保持连接…
t_en: Have to maintain the connection...

t_ch: 多么巧妙的结构…
t_en: What a wondrous structure...

t_ch: 这个世界有许多秘密…
t_en: This world has many secrets...

t_ch: 再加入一点刚毅！
t_en: Just a little more oomph!

t_ch: 第七万三千二百块…
t_en: Number <unbreak>73,200</unbreak>...

t_ch: 一定是梦…
t_en: This must be a dream...

t_ch: 什，什么
t_en: Wh—What?

t_ch: 据说这幅画记载了刻法勒将智慧传给瑟希斯的过程…反正我是不信喀。
t_en: It is said that this painting depicts the process of Kephale passing knowledge unto Cerces... I don't believe it, anyway.

t_ch: 那么小一张卷轴，怎么可能容纳得下智慧喀！
t_en: How can a scroll this size contain such wisdom!

t_ch: 这个柱子…为什么它不会被奇迹影响？为什么它没法被修好喀？
t_en: This pillar here... Why hasn't it been impacted by the miracle? Why can't it be fixed up?

t_ch: 一定是你在捣鬼吧，{RUBY_B#扎格列斯}两面窃贼{RUBY_E#}！
t_en: You're pulling my leg aren't you, {RUBY_B#Zagreus}you two-faced thief{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 这就是所谓的「黑潮」嘛，果然够劲，差点淹死我喀。
t_en: That's the so-called "black tide." It was so strong that I almost drowned.

t_ch: 感觉浑身冷冷的，有些难受…我可不想再体验一次了喀。
t_en: I felt chilled all over, so nauseous... I never want to have to experience that ever again.

t_ch: 喀，你见过泰坦们的面孔吗？很久以前曾有贤者说，不可直视神的容貌，但……
t_en: Crrk, have you seen the face of the Titans? A long time ago, a sage once said not to stare directly into the face of god, yet...

t_ch: 就拿这个兜帽人来说吧——它根本没有脸喀。
t_en: Just take this hooded guy for example — it has no face whatsoever.

t_ch: 呜啊…多谢你喀！过了这么久，总算又有人来到这里了喀。
t_en: Whoa... Thank you so much! Finally, another visitor here after all this time.

t_ch: 曾经的这里可是圣地，泰坦于此降下神谕，联盟于此商谈要事。
t_en: This place used to be a holy site where Titans delivered prophesies and alliances discussed matters of importance.

t_ch: 可惜圣地已毁，现在的天空只剩下一片寂静，再也听不见众神的声音了喀……
t_en: But unfortunately, the holy site was destroyed, and now there's just a stark silence hanging in the air. No longer may the voices of the gods be heard...

t_ch: 哎呀，好疼！
t_en: Ouch, that hurt!

t_ch: 可恶，我怎么又被埋住了？快救我出来喀！
t_en: Darn it, how have I been buried again? Quick, dig me up and save me!

t_ch: 喀喀！是{RUBY_B#扎格列斯}两面窃贼{RUBY_E#}的大手，为什么要破坏我的小家，为什么要来打扰我安逸的生活喀！
t_en: Crrk crrk! It's the big hand of {RUBY_B#Zagreus}the two-faced thief{RUBY_E#}. Why is it trashing my humble home? Why do they have to disrupt my peace and quiet?

t_ch: 不对，墙似乎是凭空消失的…难道是黑潮要来了？必须赶快溜走喀！
t_en: No, the walls just vanished out of thin air... Don't tell me that's the black tide? I need to flee! Quickly!

t_ch: 我必须背负这座天平，不然它就会缓缓倒下，就像曾经的太阳那样喀……
t_en: I must shoulder the balance of these scales, otherwise it will gradually fall, just like the sun did...

t_ch: 诶，天平的这部分其实并不会动？被我家大大骗了喀！
t_en: Wait, this part of the scale can't actually move? Boss hustled me!

t_ch: 好高啊，好可怕啊，它动起来的时候好晃悠啊……
t_en: It's so scary and high up here. And it shakes so much when it moves...

t_ch: 我只是一只虫，为什么要爬到这种地方来…我又没有翅膀喀。
t_en: I'm just a bug. Why'd I have to crawl all the way up to a place like this? It's not like I even have any wings...

t_ch: 我们的灰发勇士一路开动脑筋，终于来到了隐藏的宝箱房间喀。等待{F#她}{M#他}的将是丰厚的奖励……
t_en: Our gray-haired warrior friend racks their brains the whole way over before finally making it to the hidden treasure vault. Riches beyond {F#her}{M#his} wildest imagination await...

t_ch: 什么，你连我也不打算放过？…我们的灰发勇士坚定地走向了墙边，喜提纪念品+1喀。
t_en: What's this? You're not even letting me go, either? ...Our gray-haired warrior strides resolutely up to the wall, and get +1 Souvenir.

t_ch: 温暖的太阳在何方？柔软的死亡在何方？等待着我的爱人在何方？书写传说的英雄在何方？
t_en: Where is the warm sun? Where is soft death? Where is my lover waiting? Where are the heroes from the epics?

t_ch: 吾主啊，您何时才能归来、让大地焕发生机…喀？
t_en: My master, when will you make your return and bring life into the earth... Crrk?

t_ch: 我一只虫好孤单啊…
t_en: It's lonely being a nymph...

t_ch: 已经找到<unbreak>#1[i]</unbreak>只若虫了！
t_en: You have found <unbreak>#1[i]</unbreak> nymphs so far!

t_ch: 家人们都在这里了！
t_en: My family is all here!

t_ch: 这面墙内似乎有一只若虫。你已经想到了绝妙的捕捉方法，可惜墙缝太小，挤不进去。
t_en: There seems to be only one nymph in this wall. You came up with a brilliant way to catch it, but the crack in the wall is too small, and you can't squeeze in.

t_ch: 快给我变！
t_en: Transform for me!

t_ch: 咕噜噜~
t_en: *gurgle*

t_ch: 好可怕…
t_en: Scary...

t_ch: 好闷啊…
t_en: So boring...

t_ch: 哎哟！
t_en: Yeowch!

t_ch: 舒服…
t_en: Comfy...

t_ch: 奇怪…
t_en: Strange...

t_ch: 必须坚持住…
t_en: Gotta hang in there...

t_ch: 好高啊…
t_en: So high...

t_ch: 前有宝箱…
t_en: There's a chest up ahead...

t_ch: 我切切思慕…
t_en: I long for...

t_ch: 欢迎来到理性赐福的树庭，{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}裂分之枝{RUBY_E#}的伟大不言自明！
t_en: Welcome to the Grove, a place blessed by Reason. The majesty of the {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Bough of Rift{RUBY_E#} speaks for themself!

t_ch: 前行吧，远道的来客——你将见证绝景喀！
t_en: Onward, guest from afar — behold the gorgeous view!

t_ch: 看吧喀——伟大的吾主瑟希斯，这威严肃穆的身姿是何其高贵、何其神圣……
t_en: Observe — our great master Cerces. Such nobility and holiness in that august presence...

t_ch: 瑟希斯？在哪呢？
t_en: Cerces? Where?

t_ch: …愿仁慈的大大宽恕你的盲目与无知！我身后就有「艾格勒的启目集」，快去读读，沐浴在那位的伟大荣光之下吧喀！
t_en: ...May the master's mercy forgive your sightless ignorance! Aquila's Enlightenment lies just behind me. Read and bathe in their glorious radiance!

t_ch: 哇，竟然是瑟希斯！
t_en: Wow, it's actually Cerces!

t_ch: 对、就是这样！尽情惊叹、尽情赞美吧！那位大大就该获得如是尊崇喀！
t_en: That's right! Marvel and praise freely! Such reverence is exactly what the master deserves, crrrk!

t_ch: <size=28>啊呀！</size>
t_en: <size=28>Eek!</size>

t_ch: 别、别踩虫，虫子也是生命……
t_en: No... no stepping on bugs. Bugs are living beings, too...

t_ch: 别、别踩了！投降，投降喀！
t_en: No more, no more stepping! I yield, I yield!

t_ch: 虫可忍，但虫不可再忍。现在和解已经太晚了喀！
t_en: A bug may tolerate much, but even a bug has limits. Too late for making peace now, crrrk!

t_ch: 这只若虫一跃而起，强有力的双钳猛夹在你的膝盖上。当你意识到疼痛时，喊叫已经晚了…原来夜空中的星星这么美丽。
t_en: The nymph springs up suddenly, its strong claws gripping your knee. When you finally feel the pain, screaming is already futile... Oh, how lovely the stars look tonight.

t_ch: 诶呀——你、你怎么突然回头啊…吓死虫了喀！
t_en: Eek! Wh—Why did you turn so suddenly... you frightened me, crrrk!

t_ch: 看来这里也已经不安全了喀，还是赶紧换个地方吧喀……
t_en: It seems this spot isn't secure anymore, crrrk. Time to relocate...

t_ch: 瞧这美丽的蝶褪喀！我们的太太墨涅塔曾将它与「族亲之爱」捻成第一支金丝共同献给了瑟希斯。
t_en: Behold this lovely exuviate! Our Lady Mnestia once spun it with familial love to create the first golden thread as tribute to Cerces.

t_ch: 听说那时有无数金色蝴蝶从这空壳中飞出，洒下漫天的鳞粉，就好像金色的流星降落人间一样喀！
t_en: It's said innumerable golden butterflies burst forth from this husk, their scale dust raining down like golden stars descending to earth!

t_ch: 若能欣赏到那番美景，我的虫生该有多幸福啊喀……
t_en: What joy it would bring to see such splendor...

t_ch: 生命如此无常，不经意间就会消散。在这「理性」的庭院之中，即便生而为虫，每日也不能怠于自省喀——
t_en: Life's impermanence strikes without warning. Even as a bug in this garden of "Reason," one must maintain daily self-examination, crrk—

t_ch: 我是谁喀！我在哪喀！今天吃什么喀！
t_en: Who am I!? Where am I!? What shall I eat!?

t_ch: …喀？原来是人啊喀。我在这里藏了半天，那些怪物居然都没注意到我，难道是我太矮了？
t_en: ...Crrk? Ah, a human. Been hiding here forever, yet those monsters never spotted me. Perhaps I'm just too tiny?

t_ch: 吓死虫了，我都不敢出声喀！
t_en: Almost scared me to death, couldn't even crrk!

t_ch: 一小口…只要一小口——喝了这知识的蜜露，期末测试就不在话下了喀！趁着这里没人，赶紧……
t_en: One little sip... just a tiny taste of this Honeydew of Knowledge, and I'll ace the final exam! Now, since nobody's around...

t_ch: 不，不对…似乎不会再有期末测试了喀？
t_en: No, wait... are there any more final exams?

t_ch: 嗨，朋友——快看，我找到了个好玩的喀！
t_en: Hey buddy — check out this cool thing!

t_ch: 这个水车可真有意思，玩上一整天都不会腻喀！
t_en: This waterwheel is fascinating. I can spend forever here, crrrk!

t_ch: 瞧，刺激的来了——喀咕噜噜@#%￥…&*…
t_en: Now for the exciting part — crrk, *gurgle* @#%¥...&*...

t_ch: 瀑布后面就应该别有洞天喀！险境、奇遇、还有拔出来就能消灭所有怪物的宝剑……
t_en: Surely there's wonder behind this waterfall! Peril, quests, and a mystical blade that defeats all monsters as soon as you pull it out...

t_ch: 那些吟游诗人的故事里都是这么讲的喀！
t_en: That's how all the bards' stories go, right!?

t_ch: 感官所带来的体验已足够丰盈我们的灵魂，冰冷的理性思维只会将其扼杀，就像镰刀摧毁纯洁而脆弱的嫩芽。
t_en: Our senses alone can fulfill our souls, while cold reason only kills them, like a blade destroying pure, tender buds.

t_ch: 在真正的「美」面前，思考只是一种亵渎喀。
t_en: When facing true "beauty," any thoughts are but sacrilege, crrrk.

t_ch: 在我的威严下颤抖吧，卑微的人类！就连那些怪物都不敢靠近我，你又岂能……
t_en: Cower before my power, puny human! Even those monsters dare not approach. How could you possibly...

t_ch: 等、等等，你怎么真过来了！我错了——刚刚是在开玩笑的喀！
t_en: W—Wait, you're actually coming over! I'm sorry — I was just joking, crrrk!

t_ch: 在我的威严——
t_en: Under my majesty—

t_ch: 众所周知，「舞蹈」是法吉娜祭典上至关重要的环节。微醺的人们通过热烈地舞动肢体，进入某种近乎迷狂的状态……
t_en: It's common knowledge that "dance" plays a vital role in Phagousa's Festival. Through vigorous movement, the tipsy participants enter a near-ecstatic state...

t_ch: 而众所不知的是，这种特殊的舞蹈，其实起源于人们对法吉娜眷属的观察与模仿。
t_en: But what's less known is that this unique dance actually came from people observing and imitating Phagousa's Titankins.

t_ch: 传说酣醉时的法吉娜喜欢掀起巨浪，引领海洋生物翩然起舞，就像这幅壁画上的巨鲸…要是我也能去大海一趟就好了喀！
t_en: They say when Phagousa had a little too much to drink, they'd create massive waves and lead sea creatures in elegant dances, like the giant whale in this mural... oh how I wish I could see the ocean!

t_ch: 这池哲人莲的品相可真不错，藕节和莲子应该也都会很肥美喀。
t_en: These sage lotuses seem to be growing well. Bet their roots and seeds are nice and fat, crrk.

t_ch: 让我想想，到时候用它们调制哪一种药剂比较好喀……
t_en: Now, which concoction should I brew with these, crrrk...

t_ch: 大小近似，颜色对应，角度相同…而且都是若虫——这下就是完美的平衡了喀。是不是根本看不出差别？
t_en: Similar size, matching colors, same angle... and both are nymphs — now that's a perfect balance. Can you even tell any difference?

t_ch: 果然只有经过精确的计算，画面才能达到最完美的效果喀！
t_en: Perfect visual harmony requires precise mathematical balance, crrk!

t_ch: 喀…奇怪，这个成分…怎么是甜的喀！和资料上的完全不一样喀！
t_en: Crrrk... odd, this ingredient... it's sweet! Nothing like what the records say!

t_ch: 不行，这点数据根本得不出准确的结论，要不再尝一口吧喀……
t_en: Ugh, these data are not nearly enough for accurate conclusions. Maybe I should take one more taste, crrrk...

t_ch: 快来看看我的作品喀——你觉得我画的是什么？
t_en: Take a look at my work, crrrk. Can you guess what I drew?

t_ch: 一棵大树？
t_en: A big tree?

t_ch: 没错！但它也可以是一顶华盖、一株蘑菇……
t_en: Correct! But it could also be a canopy, a mushroom...

t_ch: 一顶华盖？
t_en: A canopy?

t_ch: 没错！但它也可以是一棵大树、一株蘑菇……
t_en: Correct! But it could also be a big tree, a mushroom...

t_ch: 一株蘑菇？
t_en: A mushroom?

t_ch: 没错！但它也可以是一棵大树、一顶华盖……
t_en: Correct! But it could also be a big tree, a canopy...

t_ch: 比起指意明确的画面，一面白墙往往反倒更具魅力！艺术的精髓就在于留白喀！
t_en: A blank wall often proves more fascinating than images with clear meanings! The essence of art lies in leaving an emptiness behind!

t_ch: 嘘——小声点，别惊扰了这些小蝶喀！
t_en: Hush now, we wouldn't wanna startle these tiny butterflies, crrk!

t_ch: 我在观察他们的蜕生过程，如果顺利的话，或许能够感悟出新知识，培养出蝶类的奇兽呢喀！
t_en: I'm studying their metamorphosis process, and if successful, might gain new knowledge to breed butterfly chimeras, crrk!

t_ch: …喀？找我有事吗？没事的话请安静，我正在为即将出版的《神悟树庭风俗考》做田野调查喀。
t_en: ...Crrrk? You need something? If not, please stay quiet, I'm conducting field studies for my upcoming Grove of Epiphany Cultural Studies.

t_ch: 这幅壁画的内容非常有趣…或许在遥远的黄金时代，这里的蝴蝶远比现在多喀！
t_en: This mural's content is fascinating... perhaps in the distant Golden Age, there were far more butterflies here than now, crrrk!

t_ch: 无论昼夜，吾都将守望这神明垂迹之地，直到吾主回归的那天。
t_en: In light and darkness, I shall guard this sacred ground, until our master's return.

t_ch: 可发生了这么多事，吾主却还是没有动静…难道它抛弃我们了吗喀？
t_en: Yet despite everything, our master shows no sign... have they forsaken us, crrk?

t_ch: 这个地方不太好过来，对吧？一路辛苦了喀！不过请相信我，朋友，这历练于你百利而无一害——
t_en: Quite a trek to get here, eh? Tough climb! But believe me, friend, this trial will serve you well—

t_ch: 只有坚持强健体魄、砥砺技艺，你的灵魂才能如肉体般坚不可摧喀！
t_en: A strong body and sharp skills make for an unbreakable spirit, crrk!

t_ch: 前有绝景——
t_en: Gorgeous view ahead—

t_ch: 伟大的吾主…
t_en: O Great Master...

t_ch: 看不到我、看不到我…
t_en: I'm invisible... I'm invisible...

t_ch: 柔软温暖的金茧…
t_en: Tender, cozy golden cocoon...

t_ch: 吾日三省吾身…
t_en: Thrice daily I reflect on myself...

t_ch: 知识的芬芳…
t_en: The sweet scent of knowledge...

t_ch: 真好玩！
t_en: So much fun!

t_ch: 咕噜噜噜噜…
t_en: *gurgle*...

t_ch: 宝藏、我的宝藏…
t_en: Precious... My precious treasure...

t_ch: 我的宝藏呢！
t_en: My treasure! Where is it!?

t_ch: 不要思考，要去感受…
t_en: Feel, don't think...

t_ch: 何人胆敢上前！
t_en: Who dares approach!

t_ch: 你见过巨鲸跳舞吗？
t_en: Ever watched a giant whale dance?

t_ch: 长势真不错啊…
t_en: Such fine growth...

t_ch: 不确定，再尝一口…
t_en: Still not sure. Need one more taste...

t_ch: 不抽象、无艺术！
t_en: No art without abstraction!

t_ch: 好漂亮的蝴蝶…
t_en: How gorgeous these butterflies are...

t_ch: …原来如此！
t_en: ...That explains it!

t_ch: 吾主啊，您何时归来…
t_en: My master, when will you return...

t_ch: 嗨！朋友，来玩啊~
t_en: Hi there! Come join me~

t_ch: 哎呦！谁啊这么缺德，砸我干什么喀！
t_en: Ouch! Hey! Who's doing this? Why attacking me!?

t_ch: 已经把塑像调整到正确的状态了…回衣匠那里看看吧。
t_en: The statue has been adjusted to the correct state... Head back to the Garmentmaker and take a look.

t_ch: 据说在工业星球，甲虫会被外表亮丽的玻璃瓶吸引…而这只若虫更爱石头塑像。
t_en: It's said that on industrial planets, beetles are often attracted by beautiful glass bottles... But this nymph prefers stone statues.

t_ch: 多么神圣的存在…
t_en: What a holy presence...

t_ch: 一束莲花开在这座塑像前，长势不错，很难想象它是如何在没有水的土地上存活的。
t_en: A cluster of lotus flowers bloomed in front of the statue. They are thriving, and it's hard to imagine how they survived on land without water.

t_ch: 这座塑像正庇护着一小片鸟巢，鸟巢的周边做了装饰，显然经常有人照料。但鸟巢的主人已不知去向。
t_en: This statue is sheltering a small bird's nest, with decorations around it. It looks well taken care of. However, the owner of the nest is nowhere to be found.

t_ch: 这座塑像旁放着一本半掩的卷轴。或许在树庭尚且热闹时，学生们会聚在它的身边读书、学习，交换知识与思考。
t_en: There's a half-opened scrolled by this statue. Perhaps, when the Grove was still bustling, students would gather around it to read, learn and exchange knowledge and ideas.

t_ch: 这座塑像的附属品像是某种用来崇神的工具，现在已经失去了光泽。
t_en: This statue's adornments appear to be tools of worship. They have since lost their luster.

t_ch: 这座塑像旁边插着一柄短矛，矛尖之锋利远胜寻常礼器。或许在某个时刻，它的主人曾确实使用过它。
t_en: A short spear is stabbed into the ground beside this statue, its edge far sharper than any ordinary ceremonial weapon. Perhaps during a certain time, its owner actually used it before.

t_ch: 这座塑像的脚边放着一口彩陶罐，历经磨损仍不失光泽，并且看上去挺有艺术水平。
t_en: A colorful dolium is placed by the feet of this statue, its sheen undiminished even with the wear of time. And its artisanship is remarkable too.

t_ch: 金苹果有着许多象征，在崇尚智慧的树庭，它便代表着智慧；但这枚苹果只是一件装饰。
t_en: The golden apple bears many symbolic meanings. In the wisdom-worshiping Grove, it represents intellect. However, this apple is merely ornamental.

t_ch: 「他的荣誉随作品一同逝去」…不知他当初做出了怎样的作品。
t_en: "His honor faded with his works." ...One can't help but wonder what he once created.

t_ch: 「与蛮邦战至力竭身亡」…树庭的勇士并不比其他城邦少。
t_en: "Died from exhaustion during the battle against the barbarian states." ...The heroic warriors of the Grove are no fewer than those of other cities.

t_ch: 「用生命探索爱的真谛」…令人钦佩的精神。
t_en: "He devoted his life to uncovering the true essence of love." ...What an admirable spirit.

t_ch: 曾属于某位贤人的塑像，因妥善维护而保存至今。时光磨去了它的细节，却无法磨灭它背后的故事。
t_en: The Sage's Statue has been preserved to this day due to proper maintenance. The years have eroded its details, but the story behind it cannot be wiped away.

t_ch: 这算「幸运」吗？
t_en: This is good luck, right?

t_ch: 精彩。相当精彩。
t_en: Wondrous. Very wondrous.

t_ch: 你战胜了生命中必然的「不幸」。你是真正的战士。
t_en: You've conquered the bad luck inevitable in life. You are a true fighter.

t_ch: 记住…不要轻视自己的选择。任何选择。
t_en: Remember... never forget the importance of your decisions. Any decision.

t_ch: 你就不怕闹出人命吗？！
t_en: Aren't you worried about causing casualties!?

t_ch: 女人露出了一个狡黠的眼神…但她的眼角很快又恢复了平整。
t_en: The lady gives you a sly look... but her expression quickly returns to normal.

t_ch: 我说过。「幸」或「不幸」是绝对的私人体验。
t_en: I already told you. Good or bad luck is all a matter of personal experience.

t_ch: 经常和命运打交道的人，自有分寸。
t_en: People who often cross paths with fate have their own standard for the two.

t_ch: 我以为最多是往宝箱里放个臭气弹…
t_en: I thought the worst that would be in the chest was a stink bomb...

t_ch: 女人向你投来怜悯的目光…但她的眼角很快又恢复了平整。
t_en: The lady looks at you with pity... but her expression quickly returns to normal.

t_ch: …你以为这是场游戏。
t_en: ...You believed this to be a game.

t_ch: 你还没意识到自己战胜了命运的摆布。
t_en: You had no idea that you bested fate's design for you.

t_ch: 看你做选择相当有趣…过程值得我细品一番。
t_en: Watching you make decisions is fascinating... The process is worth savoring.

t_ch: 哦，那个装着「幸运」的箱子——现在归你了。
t_en: Oh, the chest that contains good luck now belongs to you.

t_ch: 你是真正的抗争者。这是你自己赢来的。
t_en: You are a truly defiant soul. You have earned this prize.

t_ch: …有缘再见。
t_en: ...Until we meet again.

t_ch: 你只是一眨眼…那个神秘的女人就从眼前消失了。连一根发丝都没落下。
t_en: You blink and she's... gone. The mysterious woman has disappeared before your very eyes, vanishing without a trace.

t_ch: 真是一次…神奇的相遇。
t_en: This really was... an unusual encounter.

t_ch: 那个宝箱还在旁边，哪儿都没去。那个可能装着「幸运」的容器……
t_en: The chest is still there. It hasn't gone anywhere. There it is, a container that could be holding good luck...

t_ch: …去打开它吧。如女人所说，这是敢于与命运抗争之人应得的回报。
t_en: ...Open it up. The lady did say this is the reward for those who dare to defy fate.

t_ch: 打开小型宝箱
t_en: Open the small chest.

t_ch: 你打开了小宝箱，木板腐朽的气味扑鼻而来。
t_en: You open the small chest. The smell of rotten wood pervades your nostrils.

t_ch: 宝箱内是一方黑暗，衬得角落里闪现的一簇光芒格外惹眼。
t_en: The inside of the chest is completely black, but in the corner of the chest's lining, you see a flash of light that particularly catches your eye.

t_ch: 你将颤抖的手伸入一片漆黑中，抓住了那个——不对，是那一堆——坚硬的、发光的东西。
t_en: Your hands tremble as you reach into the pitch-black darkness. You take hold of a single — no, a pile of rigid, shining objects.

t_ch: 你将手举到眼前，仔细端详掌心里那一撮五颜六色的晶石。
t_en: You bring your hand up to your eyes and closely examine the multicolored crystals in your palm.

t_ch: ——这小小的一捧，恰好代表了一人份的「幸运」。恭喜你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: This small treasure represents enough good luck for one person. Congratulations, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我手上有一小撮晶石…
t_en: I'm holding a fistful of crystals...

t_ch: 啊？什么？你耳朵是不是不好，都说了我已经不鉴定……
t_en: Ah, what? Are you hard of hearing? I told you, I'm not an appraiser anymore...

t_ch: 喔？！这，这是……
t_en: Huh!? This... This is...

t_ch: 啧啧啧，这可是…难得一见，实在是难得一见……
t_en: Tsk tsk, this is... A rare sight. Truly a rare sight, indeed...

t_ch: 唔…你那是什么眼神？反正你就是想拿这些宝贝换钱吧？喏，这些你拿走吧，石头我就收下了——记住，这件事你可不许跟别人乱说啊！
t_en: Uh... Why are you looking at me like that? All you want anyway is to just exchange these treasures for some money, right? You take this, and I'll take the stone... Remember, don't go snitching about this deal!

t_ch: 一名古怪的男性笔直站在寒冷的铁艺栅栏门后。他的笑容十分僵硬。
t_en: An odd man stands stiffly behind the frigid iron gate. His smile is a rigid one.

t_ch: 你似乎见过他——或者他似乎见过你。
t_en: You think you've seen him before. That, or he has.

t_ch: 我们是不是见过面？
t_en: Have we met?

t_ch: 你上次把我吓到了…
t_en: You frightened the socks off me the last time...

t_ch: 您好！欢迎来到贝洛伯格的「后巷」！
t_en: Hello! Welcome to Belobog's "back alley"!

t_ch: 他的笑容十分僵硬。
t_en: His smile is stiff as a board.

t_ch: 你没事了？
t_en: You're all right now?

t_ch: 我从未发生过任何事。「后巷」不会发生任何事。
t_en: I've always been all right. Nothing ever happens in the "back alley."

t_ch: 你不是讨厌这地方吗？
t_en: I thought you hated this place.

t_ch: 我从未讨厌过「后巷」。任何人都不会讨厌「后巷」。
t_en: I've never hated the "back alley." No one hates the "back alley."

t_ch: 他的笑容十分僵硬。
t_en: His smile is stiff as a board.

t_ch: 那之前到底发生了什么？
t_en: What was going on before, then?

t_ch: 你不会是被那个东西给…
t_en: Could you have been... by the thing...

t_ch: 听我讲，{M#先生}{F#小姐}——「后巷」想要看看更大的贝洛伯格。
t_en: Listen to me, {M#mister}{F#miss}: The "back alley" wishes to see a bigger Belobog.

t_ch: 请为我们把这道铁门打开吧！
t_en: Please open the gate for us!

t_ch: 他的笑容十分僵硬。
t_en: His smile is stiff as a board.

t_ch: 当然可以！
t_en: Of course!

t_ch: 您可真是体贴！这道铁门的钥匙就在戍卫官手里。
t_en: You're a thoughtful one! The Captain has the key to this iron gate.

t_ch: 他的笑容十分僵硬。
t_en: His smile is stiff as a board.

t_en: In your mind, all memories of this "back alley" are seemingly being quickly purged. You already can't recall many details, but of one thing, you are certain...

t_en: I guarantee it.

t_en: ...Have I? Maybe. Anyway, we need to talk about why you are here...

t_ch: *只是*三天没吃饭？
t_en: JUST because I haven't eaten for three days?

t_ch: 一个人在那种暗巷里昏倒…这已经是最好的结果了哦？

t_en: Thanks for saving me, doctor.

t_ch: 这只是医生职责所在罢了。要谢还是去谢谢桑博吧。
t_en: That's my job. If you want to thank someone, you should thank Sampo.

t_ch: 不管怎么样，下次陪虎克他们一起玩的时候，不那么上纲上线也可以……
t_en: Just remember, when you play with Hook and the others next time, you don't need to play like it's a battle of wills...

t_ch: 一定要照顾好自己的身体——记好了哦。
t_en: You need to take care of yourself. Don't forget.

t_ch: 你盯上了这座广场的正中央。老实说，这里根本没有办法藏匿任何东西，或者任何人。站在这里，你只能感到自己沐浴在众人的目光中，一览无遗……
t_en: You set your sights on the center of the plaza. To be honest, it's basically impossible to hide anything or anyone here. Standing here, you can feel everyone's eyes on you...

t_ch: …但你就是喜欢*另辟蹊径*。最危险的地方就是最安全的地方——没准虎克就是发现不了你呢，对吧？
t_en: ...But you enjoy THINKING OUT OF THE BOX. The most dangerous place is often the safest. Maybe Hook won't be able to find you, right?

t_ch: 就躲在这里！
t_en: I'll hide here!

t_ch: 你迅速作出决定。所谓「大隐隐于市」，这才是经验最老到的捉迷藏玩家会选择的藏身处…至少你是这么想的。
t_en: You make a snap decision. The most experienced of hide-and-seek players will choose to hide in plain sight... Well, that's what you imagine, anyway.

t_ch: 换个地方。
t_en: Find another spot.

t_ch: （还是找找更合适的藏身处吧……）
t_en: (Maybe I should look for a better hiding spot...)
Three... two... one...

t_ch: 你已经听到虎克那欢快的声音从远处传来。接下来会发生什么呢？你止不住地想，背上竟还兴奋地沁出汗来…也许，这就是捉迷藏的乐趣所在吧。
t_en: You can already hear Hook's cheery voice echoing from afar. What's going to happen now? You can't help but imagine it as sweat rolls down your back. This exhilaration must be the joy of hide-and-seek.

t_ch: 大家都躲好了吗？我要来抓你们了哦！

t_en: Umm... Are you pretending to be a Rock Crab?

t_en: I am a Rock Crab...

t_ch: 原来你能看见我？
t_en: So you can see me?

t_en: ...I'm too shocked to talk!

t_ch: <size=36>无语了！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Too shocked to talk!</size>

t_ch: 你们怎么都不说话？
t_en: Why aren't any of you saying anything?

t_en: <size=36>Too shocked to talk!</size>

t_ch: （吓虎克一跳）
t_en: (Scare Hook)

t_ch: <size=36>哇！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Ah!</size>

t_en: <size=36>You've scared us to death!</size>

t_en: Sorry, Pitch-Dark Hook the Great...

t_ch: 要是对不起有用，还要洞洞机做什么？
t_en: If sorry does anything, why would I need Diggertron?

t_ch: 这下是我赢了！
t_en: I won!

t_ch: 什么呀！是你输了！
t_en: What? You lost!

t_ch: 荣誉队员，如果你不会玩捉迷藏，可以向我请教！虎克是不会嫌弃你笨的！
t_en: Honorary member, if you don't know how to play hide-and-seek, just ask me! I won't laugh at you for it.

t_ch: 但你现在这样，搞得虎克非常担心你的游戏体验！
t_en: But how you're acting now has me worried about your gameplay experience!

t_ch: 记住，下次一起玩的话，要向虎克问清楚规则！这次就先饶过你了！
t_en: Remember, the next time we play, ask me about the rules! I'll let you slide this time!

t_ch: 今天就先到这里——我们下次再一起玩吧！鼹鼠党，解散！各回各家睡大觉咯！
t_en: Time to call it a day. Let's play again next time! The Moles, dismissed! Go home and get some sleep!

t_en: <size=36>Go home and get some sleep!</size>

t_ch: 自从你来了贝洛伯格，便发觉自己可能与垃圾箱结下了不解之缘。现在，在捉迷藏游戏的生死关头，你再一次盯上了它。
t_en: Since arriving in Belobog, you've felt as if there is an inexplicable connection between you and dumpsters. Now, at this critical moment during hide-and-seek, you find yourself staring at the dumpster.

t_en: It's located in the plaza. It's low key and hidden. No one would expect someone to hide in there. All you need to do is hold your nose.

t_ch: 就躲在这里！
t_en: I'll hide here!

t_ch: 你迅速作出决定——玩游戏就是要赢——而你，为了赢，可以不惜一切代价！
t_en: You make a snap decision. You play to win, and no price is too high for victory!

t_ch: 换个地方。
t_en: Find another spot.

t_ch: （还是找找更合适的藏身处吧……）
t_en: (Maybe I should look for a better hiding spot...)

t_ch: 如此想着，你屏住呼吸，紧闭着双眼，翻身钻进硕大的垃圾箱，在充斥着黑暗与铁锈味的空间中蜷成一团。
t_en: With that thought in mind, you hold your breath, close your eyes, and dive into the huge dumpster. You curl up in this dark space that smells of rust.

t_ch: 居然没那么臭，你想，而且意外的舒适。会是这只枕头和这床被子的原因吗？可这里为什么会有枕头和被子？
t_en: It's not as smelly as you assumed. It's surprisingly comfortable. Is it because of the pillow and blanket? What are these doing in a dumpster anyway?

t_en: Seems odd... Does that mean you've just barged into someone's home? No time to think about all that. You just heard Hook's loud announcement!

t_ch: 这是一顶无人问津的帆布凉棚。从此处堆积的板条箱来看，这或许曾经也是一处门庭若市的小摊，只是现在不知为何惨遭废弃。
t_en: This is just an ordinary canvas awning. Judging from the stacks of crates, it might have been a stall with heavy foot traffic, but for some reason, it has been abandoned.

t_ch: 这或许是个不错的藏身点…比较传统的那种。你可以躲在板条箱后、桌子下，或者如果你另有想法——全都由你决定。
t_en: This might be a good place to hide... a more traditional spot. You can hide behind the crates or under the table. Or, you might have something else in mind. It's all up to you.

t_ch: 就躲在这里！
t_en: I'll hide here!

t_ch: 你迅速作出决定。如果一切顺利，这顶凉棚将在接下来一段时间内扮演你最坚固的庇护所。
t_en: You make a snap decision. If everything goes well, this awning will act as a trusty shelter for the time being.

t_ch: 但诚如刚才所说，即便只是这样一顶凉棚，能够藏身的地方也足以让人仔细挑上一挑了。那么，选择哪里作为藏身处会更好呢？
t_en: But, like stated before, even a simple awning offers plenty of places to hide that you can afford to pick and choose. Well, what's going to be a better spot for you to hide?

t_ch: 板条箱后面吧。
t_en: Behind the crates.

t_ch: 不错，传统且高效。于是，你伛着身子偷偷摸摸地钻进灰尘横飞的板条箱堆中……
t_en: Not bad. Traditional yet effective. And so, you bend down and stealthily move in between the crates as dust flies everywhere...

t_en: Below the table could work.

t_en: Are you serious? There's nothing blocking someone from seeing you down there. You might as well stand out in the middle of the plaza!

t_ch: ——至少那不会弄脏你的衣服。
t_en: At least you won't get your clothes dirty.

t_ch: 没有什么能拦住我！
t_en: Nothing can stop me!

t_en: That's good. Rise to the challenge! You crouch down and try to get underneath the table...

t_en: Uh-oh. This space is a little too narrow for you. Here's an apology on behalf of the table.

t_ch: 不错，思路清奇。于是，你挺起腰板，站到了桌子后……

t_ch: 老实说，你这姿态着实有些奇怪…如果要扮演商人，你总该吼两嗓子，佯装叫卖一下吧？
t_en: To be fair, there's something off about how you're doing this... If you're pretending to be a merchant, you should have to hawk a bit — maybe act like you are selling something?

t_ch: 但遗憾的是，你手边也没有任何可以出卖的东西……
t_en: Unfortunately, you don't have anything on hand to sell...

t_ch: 哈哈！这下可糟糕咯。
t_en: Haha! This isn't going to end well.

t_ch: 现在躲到箱子后还来得及吗？
t_en: Is there enough time to hide behind some crates?

t_ch: 当然来得及！于是，你伛着身子偷偷摸摸地钻进灰尘横飞的板条箱堆中……
t_en: Of course there's enough time! You crouch down and stealthily move in between the crates as dust flies everywhere...

t_ch: 我可以卖艺啊！
t_en: I could do some busking!

t_ch: 卖艺？什么…等等！你先别着急喊出来！
t_en: Busking? What... Wait! Don't shout that!

t_ch: 可惜晚了…你这一句发自内心的呼喊立刻吸引来众多诧异的目光——
t_en: Sadly, it's too late... The words rattling in your head got out and immediately attracted everyone's astonished gaze.

t_ch: <size=36>喂！那不是拯救了下层区的大英雄吗！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Hey! You're that hero who saved the Underworld!</size>

t_ch: 你没有想过事态会向这个方向发展…最初的你不过想在捉迷藏游戏中另辟蹊径——可现在竟演变成一场轰动磐岩镇的「大英雄握手会」了！
t_en: You hadn't expected things to go this way... You were just hoping to take an unconventional approach in hide-and-seek. Now your attempt has turned into a sensational "Meet the Hero" event in Boulder Town!

t_ch: 一小时、两小时…你从没想过下层区竟然有这么多居民！他们支付信用点，满怀感激或敬意，又或是纯粹来凑热闹，排队捧起你的手，视之若珍宝，反复摩挲……
t_en: One hour, two hours... You had no idea there were so many people who lived in the Underworld! They paid in credit and came up to shake your hand, either due to being grateful, respectful, or just there to join the excitement. They viewed it as a rare opportunity and continually patted your hand...

t_ch: 而在「握手会」的第四个钟头，这场欢闹的盛会终于迎来了尾声——
t_en: The commotion finally wore down by the fourth hour of this "Meet the Hero" event.

t_ch: 人群散去。你打量着自己的双手：这对紧握球棍、历战无算的无情铁手，如今仿佛被里里外外仔细抛了光…很可能比大矿区的地髓还要亮。
t_en: The crowd has dissipated. You take stock of your two hands. These invincible hands have held on to the baseball bat and fought so many battles, but now they look like they've been carefully polished to shine... They might be even shinier than the Geomarrow in the Great Mine.

t_ch: 可鼹鼠党的捉迷藏游戏怎么办？你已经不是很在乎了——毕竟那帮小朋友也才和你握过手呢！
t_en: What about The Moles' game of hide-and-seek? You don't really care at this point — those kids just shook your hand too!

t_ch: 出于良心，你没有收他们的钱。友情可是无价的！
t_en: Out of the kindness of your heart, you opt to not take their money. Friendship is priceless after all!
Three... two... one...

t_ch: 你已经听到虎克那欢快的声音从远处传来，可你根本没有选好合适的藏身处！
t_en: You can hear Hook's excited voice from afar off, but you haven't chosen a suitable place to hide yet!

t_ch: 你感到恐惧，怔在原地。接下来会发生什么呢？你止不住地想，背上竟还兴奋地沁出汗来…也许，这就是捉迷藏的乐趣所在吧。
t_en: You feel afraid. You are frozen in place. What's going to happen now? You can't help but think about it. Sweat runs down your back as you get excited... This exhilaration must be the joy of hide-and-seek.

t_ch: 大家都躲好了吗？我要来抓你们了哦！
t_en: Ready or not, here I come!

t_ch: <size=36>……</size>
t_en: <size=36>...</size>

t_ch: （适当的沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: …荣誉队员。
t_en: ...Honorary member.

t_ch: 那个…你是在演岩居蟹吗？
t_en: Umm... Are you pretending to be a Rock Crab?

t_ch: 我确实是一只岩居蟹…
t_en: I am a Rock Crab...

t_ch: 原来你能看见我？
t_en: So you can see me?

t_ch: …虎克已经无语了！
t_en: ...I'm too shocked to talk!

t_ch: <size=36>无语了！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Too shocked to talk!</size>

t_ch: 你们怎么都不说话？
t_en: Why aren't any of you saying anything?

t_ch: …虎克已经无语了！
t_en: ...I'm too shocked to talk!

t_ch: <size=36>无语了！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Too shocked to talk!</size>

t_ch: （吓虎克一跳）
t_en: (Scare Hook)

t_ch: <size=36>哇！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Ah!</size>

t_ch: 你干嘛呀荣誉队员！吓死我了！
t_en: What are you doing, honorary member? You've scared me to death!

t_ch: <size=36>吓死我们了！</size>
t_en: <size=36>You've scared us to death!</size>

t_ch: 对不起，漆黑的虎克大人…
t_en: Sorry, Pitch-Dark Hook the Great...

t_ch: 要是对不起有用，还要洞洞机做什么？
t_en: If sorry does anything, why would I need Diggertron?

t_ch: 这下是我赢了！
t_en: I won!

t_ch: 这都什么跟什么呀！是你输了！
t_en: What are you even talking about? You lost!

t_ch: 荣誉队员，如果你不会玩捉迷藏，可以向我请教！虎克是不会嫌弃你笨的！
t_en: Honorary member, if you don't know how to play hide-and-seek, just ask me! I won't laugh at you for it.

t_ch: 但你现在这样，搞得虎克非常担心你的游戏体验！
t_en: But how you're acting now has me worried about your gameplay experience!

t_ch: 记住，下次一起玩的话，要向虎克问清楚规则！这次就先饶过你了！
t_en: Remember, the next time we play, ask me about the rules! I'll let you slide this time!

t_ch: 今天就先到这里——我们下次再一起玩吧！鼹鼠党，解散！各回各家睡大觉咯！
t_en: Time to call it a day. Let's play again next time! The Moles, dismissed! Go home and get some sleep!

t_ch: <size=36>回家睡大觉咯！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Go home and get some sleep!</size>

t_ch: 你再一次站在地髓矿脉的面前——它哪儿也没去，就这么默默地等着你回来。令人感动。
t_en: Once more, you stand before the Geomarrow vein — it's right where you left it, silently waiting for your return. How touching.

t_ch: 你为自己争取到了又一次机会，新晋矿工{NICKNAME}。别浪费它。
t_en: You've earned yourself another chance, rookie miner. Don't waste it, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 用尽全身力气敲下去。
t_en: You throw all of your strength into striking the rock.

t_ch: 停——冷静点，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Stop — calm down, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 维修一把矿镐可能很容易，但想重建人与人之间破裂的信任——不可能，简直难如登天。
t_en: It might be easy enough to mend a pickax, but trying to repair the broken trust between people is as impossible as touching the sky.

t_ch: 登天？听上去很简单…
t_en: Touching the sky? Sounds easy enough...

t_ch: 别抖机灵，{NICKNAME}。你知道这是什么意思。
t_en: Don't get ahead of yourself, {NICKNAME}. You know what this means.

t_ch: 现在，冷静下来，放下你手里的矿镐…再选一次。乖。
t_en: Now, calm down and put down your pickax... Be good and pick a different option.

t_ch: 「乖，小东西。」
t_en: "Be good, little one."

t_ch: 用*十分*适中的力气敲下去。
t_en: You use an entirely appropriate amount of strength when striking down.

t_ch: 噹！
t_en: Dong!

t_ch: ——赞美琥珀王！你从来没干过类似的活，但这一击的力度简直完美如梦境…也许你天生就是干这行的料。
t_en: Praise the Amber Lord! You have never done anything like this, but you used the absolutely perfect amount of strength... Maybe you have found your life calling.

t_ch: 一块拳头大小的地髓结晶落在你的脚边。它的形状近似正十二面体，颜色橙红剔透，核芯周围甚至还反射出几抹靛蓝。
t_en: A Geomarrow crystal the size of a fist falls next to your feet. It has a shape similar to a dodecahedron, with a clear, orange-red color. There are even some shocks of indigo that radiate from the core of the stone.

t_ch: 不需要冗杂的鉴定步骤，你内心已经十分清楚——这下发财了。
t_en: A rock like this doesn't need complex appraisal. You know full well what this is — big money.

t_ch: 你抬头看着那片雄伟的矿脉…橙色的面容缓和慈祥。仔细聆听…它似乎对这次完美的挥镐发表了自己的看法。
t_en: You look up at the majestic vein... The orange surface has a warmth and kindness to it... Listening closely, you can almost hear it give its own take on your perfect swing.

t_ch: 「干得好，小东西。」
t_en: "Well done, little one."

t_ch: 你对着自然的伟大显像轻鞠一躬。现在，回去找那个女工头，让她帮忙鉴定这块地髓结晶的价值吧。
t_en: You give a slight bow to this marvel of nature. Now, it's time to find the supervisor and have her help appraise how much this chunk of Geomarrow crystal is worth.

t_ch: 轻轻地敲下去。
t_en: Gently tap downward.

t_ch: 叮！
t_en: Ding!
"Ding"?

t_ch: 然后呢？
t_en: Go on.

t_ch: 「叮」——这就是全部了，没有下文。你在期待些什么？
t_en: "Ding." That's it. There's nothing else. What were you expecting?

t_ch: 你抬头看着那片雄伟的矿脉…橙色的面容缓和慈祥，但你还是觉察到了它藏在表面之下的嘲弄：
t_en: You look up and see the magnificent vein... Its surface gives off a warm, kind orange glow, but you still notice the mockery beneath its surface.

t_ch: 「没吃饭吗，小东西？」
t_en: "You didn't eat a good meal, little one?"

t_ch: 你羞恼不已。再给你一次机会，你一定会给它点颜色瞧瞧。
t_en: You are embarrassed. If you are given a second chance, you'll teach it a lesson.

t_ch: 又见面了，外来者。怎么说，要谈生意，还是说你找我有事？
t_en: Hello again, outsider. What brings you here, business or something personal?

t_ch: 我找到了这个。
t_en: I found this.

t_ch: 嗯？你挖到什么好东西了吗？给我瞅瞅。
t_en: Eh? Dug up anything good? Let me see.

t_ch: 喔，这是……
t_en: Oh, this is...

t_ch: 看品相，这地髓结晶的纯净度应该至少在<unbreak>85%</unbreak>以上…这要是被直接扔进锅炉里烧成灰渣，可就太浪费了。
t_en: Judging by the appearance, the purity of this Geomarrow crystal has to be at least <unbreak>85%</unbreak>... Chucking it in the boiler and burning it down to slag would be too wasteful.

t_ch: 要我说，{NICKNAME}，你找到的这块东西，只要稍稍加工一下就能直接安到上层区的永冬铭碑上——一般人保准看不出区别！
t_en: If you ask me, {NICKNAME}, this thing you found just needs a little refinement before being put directly onto the Everwinter Monument up in the Overworld — Nobody would be able to tell the difference!

t_ch: 对了，你手上不是正缺钱吗？把这块结晶卖给我，怎么样？价格绝对公道。你去打听打听，磐岩镇还有比安东尼娜出手更大方的矿监吗？
t_en: By the way, aren't you low on funds at the moment? How about you sell me this crystal, huh? I'll give you a fair price. Go ask around, and you won't find a more generous supervisor around here than me!
